summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Machine policy for KMS client online AVS validation data. Good for privacy-sensitive or tightly controlled environments, but it opts the device out of automatically sending KMS activation-state data to Microsoft.

setting.name: KMS Client Online AVS Validation
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can send KMS client activation-state data to Microsoft during activation. This policy decides whether that data is sent automatically.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable this data flow when they want a stricter activation privacy posture or need tighter control over what activation-related information is sent to Microsoft.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off reduces activation-related data sent to Microsoft, but it also opts the device out of the normal automatic online validation data flow described by Microsoft's policy text.
setting.professional_notes: AVSValidationGP.admx maps the machine policy named NoAcquireGT to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Software Protection Platform\NoGenTicket with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents the computer from sending KMS client activation data to Microsoft automatically.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: KMS client activation data is automatically sent to Microsoft when the device activates, according to Microsoft's local policy help text.

allowed_values.label: KMS activation data sent automatically
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and KMS client activation data is automatically sent to Microsoft.

allowed_values.label: KMS activation data not sent automatically
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and the computer opts out of automatically sending KMS client activation data to Microsoft.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoGenTicket = 1, which matches the documented opt-out behavior from the local Microsoft policy text.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 and newer systems carrying the Software Protection Platform AVS validation policy

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The local Microsoft ADML help text explicitly says that if the policy is disabled or not configured, KMS client activation data will be sent to Microsoft services when the device activates.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that should keep the normal KMS client activation data flow
avoid_for: Privacy-sensitive or tightly controlled activation environments
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its normal activation-related data flow described by Microsoft's policy text.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to opt out of automatically sending KMS activation data to Microsoft.
tradeoffs: The device keeps the default automatic activation-data behavior.
No policy-based restriction is enforced for this activation data flow.

recommended_profiles.label: Privacy focused
avoid_for: Setups that prefer the standard automatic online validation data path

states.rationale: Enable the policy that opts the device out of automatically sending KMS client activation data to Microsoft.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a stricter posture around KMS activation-state data being sent to Microsoft.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the standard automatic online AVS validation data behavior.
tradeoffs: Activation-related data sent to Microsoft is reduced for this path.
The device no longer follows the default automatic KMS client online AVS validation behavior.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AVSValidationGP.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file defines the NoAcquireGT machine policy and maps it to NoGenTicket with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the Software Protection Platform policy key.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AVSValidationGP.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents the computer from sending KMS client activation data to Microsoft automatically, while disabled or not configured sends the data when the device activates.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ICM.admx reference bundle
evidence.summary: The local ICM policy bundle also references NoGenTicket with 1 and 0 values, reinforcing the registry mapping used by the policy set.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoGenTicket = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft AVSValidationGP policy files explicitly define the machine policy path HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Software Protection Platform, the value name NoGenTicket, and the enabled and disabled behavior. The app writes the documented enabled-state value 1 that opts the device out of automatically sending KMS activation data to Microsoft.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="NoAcquireGT" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Software Protection Platform" valueName="NoGenTicket"> <enabledValue><decimal value = "1" /></enabledValue> <disabledValue><decimal value = "0" /></disabledValue>; AVSValidationGP.adml: "Enabled (data will not be sent to Microsoft)".
validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file defines the exact machine policy path and value name used by the app, with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The paired ADML help text explains that enabling the policy opts the device out of automatically sending KMS client activation data to Microsoft.
